data.query: Propafenone AND manic bipolar affective disorder

results.title: Adverse effects of interactions between antipsychotics and medications used in the treatment of cardiovascular disorders.
results.abstract: High level of comorbidity between bipolar disorder or schizophrenia and cardiovascular diseases (CVD) in clinical practice may contribute to drug-drug interactions between medications used in these conditions. The aim of this study was to evaluate harmful interactions between antipsychotics and medications used in treatment of CVD.
mesh_terms: Drug-Related Side Effects and Adverse Reactions

results.title: An organic psychosis due to a venlafaxine-propafenone interaction.
results.abstract: An extraordinary case of an organic psychosis during the treatment of a patient with a bipolar affective disorder.
results.journal: International journal of psychiatry in medicine

results.title: A case of mania secondary to propafenone.
results.abstract: A case of mania secondary to the administration of a new antiarrhythmic, propafenone, is described. This is a previously unrecognized potential complication in the use of this agent. Since propafenone is chemically related to a new antidepressant, bupropion, it may possess some antidepressant activity and thus have the potential to produce affective psychiatric side effects.
